pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: H1 ŌĆö `formatMonthlyProgressEmail` mock uses `unknown[]` for `ChildStruggleLine[]
body:
**H1 ŌĆö `formatMonthlyProgressEmail` mock uses `unknown[]` for `ChildStruggleLine[]`**
`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.test.ts` lines 160, 175

The real signature (`notifications.ts:387`) takes `struggleLines: ChildStruggleLine[]` as the third parameter. The mock stubs it as `unknown[]`, meaning any array shape is silently accepted. Future changes to `ChildStruggleLine` that break the call-site won't be caught here. Fix: use `ChildStruggleLine[]` directly, or `Parameters<typeof formatMonthlyProgressEmail>[2]` to track the real type automatically.

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: H2 ŌĆö `startSession` double-cast bypasses a real type incompatibility
body:
**H2 ŌĆö `startSession` double-cast bypasses a real type incompatibility**
`apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.test.ts:184`

`startSession as unknown as typeof import('./session-crud').startSession` silently papers over a genuine mismatch between the spy's inferred type and the imported function's signature. The double-cast through `unknown` prevents TypeScript from ever surfacing drift between the spy and the real implementation. Worth surfacing the underlying error to confirm this is a Jest inference quirk (acceptable) rather than an API shape divergence (bug). Add a comment if the cast is intentional.

---

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: H1: `AppVariables.profileId` typed `string` but middleware sets `string | undefined
body:
**H1: `AppVariables.profileId` typed `string` but middleware sets `string | undefined`**

`profile-scope.ts:51` explicitly declares `profileId: string | undefined`. The new `AppVariables` in `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:11` declares `profileId: string` (no undefined). This is wrong from day one. Currently only used in tests so no runtime breakage, but if any production route is ever parameterized with `AppEnv`, it will trust `string` for what is actually `string | undefined` ŌĆö exactly the gap that `requireProfileId()` exists to close.

Fix: `profileId: string | undefined;` matching `ProfileScopeEnv`.

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: H2: `session-operations.integration.test.ts:306` ŌĆö raw cast papers over a return-type lie
body:
**H2: `session-operations.integration.test.ts:306` ŌĆö raw cast papers over a return-type lie**

```ts
const firstMeta = firstResult.metadata as Record<string, unknown>;
```

`syncHomeworkState` is declared to return `Promise<{ metadata: HomeworkSessionMetadata }>` but actually returns fields (`loggedCorrectionIds`, `loggedStartedProblemIds`, `loggedCompletedProblemIds`) absent from `HomeworkSessionMetadata`. The test was correctly asserting those fields before this PR; the fix papers over the type gap with a raw cast instead of fixing the declared return type.

The cast means future callers will trust the too-narrow annotation and silently lose the extra fields. Options: (1) widen the return type of `syncHomeworkState`; (2) add a `// TODO:` comment referencing a tracking issue if deferring. Do not leave an unsignposted `as Record<string, unknown>` in an integration test that verifies a correctness invariant.

---

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: M1 ŌĆö `BASE_ENV: Env` fixture uses post-coercion numeric literals
body:
**M1 ŌĆö `BASE_ENV: Env` fixture uses post-coercion numeric literals**
`apps/api/src/config.test.ts:28-30`

`MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_THRESHOLD: 0.15`, `MAX_DEDUP_LLM_CALLS_PER_SESSION: 10`, and `MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_ROLLOUT_PCT: 0` are JS numbers. In production they arrive as strings from the CF env. `Env` is the Zod output type (post-`z.coerce`), so TypeScript accepts these. The gap: `validateProductionKeys` is never tested with raw string inputs for these keys. A raw string that fails `z.coerce.number()` in a corner case would not be caught. Consider adding one test that pipes a `Record<string, string>` through `validateEnv` to exercise the coercion path.

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: M2 ŌĆö `PromiseLike.then` reassignment is fragile (pre-existing)
body:
**M2 ŌĆö `PromiseLike.then` reassignment is fragile (pre-existing)**
`apps/api/src/services/book-suggestion-generation.test.ts:112`

`PromiseLike<unknown[]>` is technically more correct than `Promise<unknown[]>` for a patched thenable (PromiseLike allows null callbacks per the ES spec). The actual implementation is unchanged. Pre-existing fragility not worsened by this PR ŌĆö the mock chain still lacks `.catch`/`.finally` ŌĆö but worth a future cleanup note.

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: M3 ŌĆö `event.name` removal in `review-calibration-grade.test.ts` is correct
body:
**M3 ŌĆö `event.name` removal in `review-calibration-grade.test.ts` is correct**
`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/review-calibration-grade.test.ts:8`

This is correct ŌĆö `handleReviewCalibrationGrade` types its `event` param as `{ data: unknown }` and never reads `name`. No action needed; noting here so a future reviewer does not re-add it.

---

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: M1: `topup-expiry-reminder.test.ts:201,245` ŌĆö `(args.length as number)` cast is a no-op
body:
**M1: `topup-expiry-reminder.test.ts:201,245` ŌĆö `(args.length as number)` cast is a no-op**

`ConstructorParameters<typeof Date>` is a rest-parameter tuple. In TS strict mode, comparing `.length` to `0` triggers TS2367 because the inferred type is a union of literal lengths. Casting to `number` silences this without addressing it. A future TS upgrade may re-surface the error. Use `!args.length` instead.

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: M2: `export.test.ts:316,325` ŌĆö non-null assertions on optional field hide future failures
body:
**M2: `export.test.ts:316,325` ŌĆö non-null assertions on optional field hide future failures**

`result.sessionEvents!.find(...)` assumes the mock always populates the optional field. Add `expect(result.sessionEvents).toBeDefined()` before the `!` accesses so a mock-regression produces a readable Jest failure instead of an obscure TypeError.

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: M3: `session-operations.integration.test.ts` ŌĆö non-narrowing assertion before type cast

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 ŌĆö TS2769/2339/2353/2345/2352/2322 ŌĆö heterogeneous test type fixes (~50 errors / ~24 files).
title: File**: `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:13
body:
**File**: `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:13`

`profileId` was correctly fixed to `string | undefined` (CR-1) because the middleware only sets it when a profile resolves. `subscriptionId` has the identical problem: `meteringMiddleware` is the only setter and it is **not registered on every route**. Every test file that imports `Hono<{ Variables: AppVariables }>` without the metering middleware (`account.test.ts`, `profile-scope.test.ts`, `notes.test.ts`, `stripe-webhook.test.ts`) has TypeScript believing `c.get('subscriptionId')` returns `string` when the runtime value is `undefined`.

The prior review deferred this under CR-2 ("partial consolidation") but CR-2 is about fields *not yet merged into AppVariables*; the type-safety issue with fields that ARE present is distinct and the same class as CR-1. Fix: change `subscriptionId: string` to `subscriptionId: string | undefined`, and `llmTier: LLMTier` to `llmTier: LLMTier | undefined` (metering sets both together).

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: GC1 exemptions need stronger justification.** `weekly-self-reports.test.ts` and `weekly-progress-push.test.ts` each introduce 6-7 new `jest.mock` calls on internal relative-path modules, all tagged `g
body:
**GC1 exemptions need stronger justification.** `weekly-self-reports.test.ts` and `weekly-progress-push.test.ts` each introduce 6-7 new `jest.mock` calls on internal relative-path modules, all tagged `gc1-allow: unit test boundary`. CLAUDE.md says _"review will check the reason"_. The current reason is too generic. The mocked modules ŌĆö `../../services/practice-activity-summary`, `../../services/snapshot-aggregation`, `../../services/weekly-report`, `../../services/solo-progress-reports` ŌĆö are the exact services these Inngest functions orchestrate. Mocking them means the cron tests verify glue code, not real behaviour. Integration test coverage already exists nearby for each of these services. Either convert these to integration tests (preferred per the GC1 rationale) or spell out why integration is infeasible (e.g. "Inngest step runner cannot execute without the full emulator"). See inline comments.

---

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: Unvalidated `subjectId` in dictation and quiz routes
body:
**Unvalidated `subjectId` in dictation and quiz routes**
See inline on `packages/schemas/src/dictation.ts:76`. The `subjectId` field accepted in `POST /v1/dictation/results` (and for non-vocabulary quiz rounds in `orchestrate-round.ts:93`) is stored in `practice_activity_events.subject_id` without verifying the subject belongs to the caller's profile. CLAUDE.md rule: "Writes must include explicit profileId protection or verify ownership through the parent chain." The practical impact is limited (subject name resolves to `null` in summaries via the join's `eq(subjects.profileId, profileId)` condition), but the FK can point to another profile's subject row, which is a data-integrity violation. A one-line scoped-repo ownership check before the write closes this.

---

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 29
body:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 29

Drizzle's `PgTransaction` has a narrower surface than `NodePgDatabase`. The double cast erases the type mismatch at compile time. The same pattern exists in `quiz/complete-round.ts`, but that is not a reason to widen it. If the refactor is out of scope here, add a comment linking to a tracked issue.

---

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 45
body:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 45

`localDate` is a calendar date in the learner's local timezone. Appending `T00:00:00.000Z` pins it to UTC midnight, which misrepresents the event for every learner outside UTC. A learner in UTC+9 who practices on May 13 local time gets a `completedAt` of May 12 15:00 UTC, causing report aggregations to bucket the event into the wrong day.

---

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: File:** `apps/api/src/services/practice-activity-events.ts` line 32
body:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/practice-activity-events.ts` line 32

```ts
return `${activityType}:${sourceType}${subtype}:${sourceId}${occurrence}`;
```

When `activitySubtype` is present the subtype is spliced into the string adjacent to `sourceType` without a separator between them. Two different `(sourceType, activitySubtype)` pairs can produce the same key if `sourceType` ever contains a colon. Today's source types are safe, but the format is fragile. A fixed-field scheme with explicit separators for every segment (including a sentinel when `activitySubtype` is null) is more robust.

---

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: File:** `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts` line 102
body:
**File:** `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts` line 102

`/[?!.:]/.test(text)` returns `true` for any period, which matches abbreviations (`Dr.`), URLs, and ordinary prose. This classifies nearly all multi-word text as having homework cues, suppressing escalation even for weak-confidence results with no actual homework content. Restricting to `?` and `!` only, or requiring whitespace after a period, would reduce false-positive suppression.

---

### ­¤¤ó Low ŌĆö 90-day scan on Progress tab critical path

**File:** `apps/api/src/services/progress.ts`

`getPracticeActivitySummary` is parallelised with the subjects query, so it does not block the critical path, but a 90-day window will grow as the table fills. Consider reducing to 30 days for the overview and reserving 90 days for report-detail endpoints that are opened intentionally.

---

### ­¤¤ó Low ŌĆö `emptyProgressOverviewPracticeSummary` duplicates test fixture

`packages/schemas/src/progress.ts` defines this object inline; it is structurally identical to `emptyPracticeActivitySummary` in `apps/api/src/test-utils/practice-activity-summary-fixture.ts`. Exporting one canonical empty from `@eduagent/schemas` and importing it in the test fixture would keep them in sync automatically.

---

### Ō£ģ Strengths
- Migration SQL: idempotent DDL, `NOT VALID` backfill, correct `NULLIF` RLS guard pattern.
- Deduplication via `(profile_id, dedupe_key)` unique index; integration tests verify constraint behaviour directly.
- All `practice_activity_events` writes are inside the parent transaction (dictation, quiz), keeping events atomic with the primary record.
- `@eduagent/schemas` owns all shared types; no local API-facing type redefinitions.
- `unregisterProvider` + `dispose()` on the LLM test fixture cleanly fixes provider leakage between integration tests.
- Locale key-parity test is a good CI guard for i18n drift.

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: Colon-collision in `buildCelebrationDedupeKey`** ŌĆö see inline on `celebration-events.ts:25
body:
**1. Colon-collision in `buildCelebrationDedupeKey`** ŌĆö see inline on `celebration-events.ts:25`
The separator character `:` is not encoded, so distinct `(celebrationType, reason, sourceId)` tuples can produce the same key if any field contains a literal colon. `buildPracticeActivityDedupeKey` in the sibling service already uses `encodeURIComponent`. Both helpers should apply the same encoding.

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: Telemetry write inside transaction with no error isolation** ŌĆö see inline on `assessments.ts:172ŌĆō178
body:
**2. Telemetry write inside transaction with no error isolation** ŌĆö see inline on `assessments.ts:172ŌĆō178`
`recordAssessmentCompletionActivity` is called inside the Drizzle transaction. While `onConflictDoNothing` handles the expected duplicate path, any other unexpected DB failure will roll back the entire assessment update and surface a 500. The session-exchange path wraps the equivalent call with `captureException` and lets the exchange complete. Activity event recording is telemetry; it should not gate the core completion flow.

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: practice_activity_events.source_type` has no DB-level constraint.** Unlike `celebration_events`, which gets the `source_type_known` CHECK constraint in migration 0074, `practice_activity_events.source
body:
**`practice_activity_events.source_type` has no DB-level constraint.** Unlike `celebration_events`, which gets the `source_type_known` CHECK constraint in migration 0074, `practice_activity_events.source_type` is unconstrained text. Valid values today are `assessment`, `dictation_result`, `quiz_round`, `quiz_mastery_item`, `session_event`. A future caller with a typo will silently insert an invalid source type, breaking any report query that groups or filters on that column. A CHECK constraint (or at minimum a shared constant between the migration and the service layer) would catch this at insert time.

**OCR endpoint buffers the full body before validating size.** `c.req.parseBody()` reads the entire multipart request into worker memory before the `file.size > OCR_CONSTRAINTS.maxFileSizeBytes` check. A client that sends a 50 MB body exhausts CF Worker memory before being rejected. Enforce the limit via a Hono body-size middleware or CF's `limits.fetch_body_size_limit` in `wrangler.toml`. See inline comment on `homework.ts`.

**`NOT VALID` in migration 0074 lacks a rationale comment.** `NOT VALID` is appropriate when a table already has data you cannot afford to scan. `celebration_events` was created in migration 0072 ŌĆö if you used `NOT VALID` because rows were written on staging between the two deployments, that context should be in a SQL comment so the next reader does not wonder whether existing rows violate the constraint.

**Inconsistent `dedupeKey` format at the `recitation`/`fluency_drill` call-site.** In `session-exchange.ts` the recitation event uses a hand-rolled key that bypasses `buildPracticeActivityDedupeKey`, while the fluency_drill event on the adjacent branch uses the auto-generated format. Both reference the same `aiEventId` and are unique, so there is no correctness bug today, but the two formats diverge in the DB and will confuse anyone auditing or migrating dedupe keys. Pick one approach for both.

---

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: Silent practice-event drop has no queryable failure rate** (inline on `apps/api/src/routes/assessments.ts:174`)
body:
**Silent practice-event drop has no queryable failure rate** (inline on `apps/api/src/routes/assessments.ts:174`)
The `try/catch` around `recordAssessmentCompletionActivity` fires `captureException` but Sentry exceptions are sampled. Per CLAUDE.md: "if you can't query how many times the fallback fired in the last 24 hours, the 'recovery' is invisible." A tagged metric or an Inngest retry is needed.

**New OCR error string not i18n'd** (inline on `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts:399`)
Two occurrences of `"We couldn't read that clearly..."` are hardcoded in English. The PR adds locale files for six languages. These strings must go through the translation system like `NON_HOMEWORK_ERROR_MESSAGE`'s sibling keys.

**Migration 0074 missing rollback section** (inline on `apps/api/drizzle/0074_celebration_events_source_type_check.sql`)
CLAUDE.md requires any migration altering an existing table to have a Rollback section in the plan. This migration adds a `NOT VALID` constraint to `celebration_events`; rollback is straightforward (`DROP CONSTRAINT`) but must be documented before the migration runs on staging.

---
